sources.title: NOAA Fisheries — Satellite Monitoring of Cook Inlet Beluga Whales (GAIA)
sources.note: GAIA method (machine learning + very-high-resolution satellite imagery + expert annotation), the June 2025 tasking that yielded no imagery (likely competing priorities near the Port of Anchorage and JBER), partner list, EarthExplorer API. Page updated June 2026.

sources.title: NOAA Fisheries — Geospatial Artificial Intelligence For Animals (GAIA)
sources.note: GAIA integrates VHR satellite imagery with cloud computing, geospatial analysis, and machine learning; automates imagery retrieval via the USGS EarthExplorer API.

sources.title: NOAA Fisheries — Beluga Whale species page
sources.note: Cook Inlet population ESA-listed endangered in 2008; declined ~80% from ~1,300 (1979) to ~279 (2018); best current estimate 331, may be stabilizing.

sources.title: NOAA Fisheries — New abundance estimate for endangered Cook Inlet beluga whales
sources.note: 331 best estimate from the 2022 aerial survey (range 290 to 386), an aerial-survey result not an AI output; Kim Goetz (AFSC) cautions estimates vary year to year.
